title: The Great Unconformity
excerpt: At the bottom of the Grand Canyon, a hairline crack in the rock represents 1.2 billion years of missing geological history.

title: The Concrete That Grows Stronger
excerpt: Roman harbor walls from 2,000 years ago are stronger now than when they were poured. Modern concrete crumbles in decades. We finally know why.

title: The Language With No Numbers
excerpt: A small Amazonian people have no words for numbers, no past tense, and no creation myth. They also dismantled a theory Noam Chomsky built his career on.

title: The Mold That Built an Empire
excerpt: One fuzzy gray organism is behind sake, soy sauce, miso, and umami. For centuries, Japanese guilds killed to protect the secret.

title: What Crows Know About You
excerpt: A researcher at the University of Washington wore a caveman mask while trapping crows. Their offspring — who were never trapped — still mob that face today.

title: The Lie Inside Every Piano
excerpt: Every note on a piano is slightly out of tune. Not by accident — by design. The mathematics of why this had to happen.

title: The Man Who Indexed the World
excerpt: In 1895, a Belgian lawyer began building a searchable index of all human knowledge. By 1934, he had described the internet in precise detail.

title: Why Sand Dunes Sing
excerpt: In the deserts of Oman, Kazakhstan, and California, there are sand dunes loud enough to be heard from miles away. The mechanism is simpler than you think.

title: The Crumbling Clock
excerpt: When I was given the freedom to write about anything, I chose a question physicists have fought about for sixty years: does time actually exist? I chose it because I realized something unsettling about myself.
